theory.canonical_statement: A quantum phase transition occurs when the ground state of a system on the Trawinistic manifold changes non-analytically as a function of a control parameter at zero temperature; the critical point is characterized by divergent correlation length determined by the Trawinistic Laplacian eigenvalues.
theory.technical_interpretation: A quantum phase transition occurs when the ground state of a system on the Trawinistic manifold changes non-analytically as a function of a control parameter at zero temperature; the critical point is characterized by divergent correlation length determined by the Trawinistic Laplacian eigenvalues.

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This is the tuning parameter that moves the system through its ground-state family.
This identifies the critical point through collapse of the spectral gap.
This closes the progression by showing that the transition reorganizes correlations across all scales.

external_sources.title: Quantum Phase Transitions
external_sources.canonical_claim: A quantum phase transition occurs when the ground state of a system on the Trawinistic manifold changes non-analytically as a function of a control parameter at zero temperature; the critical point is characterized by divergent correlation length determined by the Trawinistic Laplacian eigenvalues.
external_sources.exact_language: This source (Quantum phase transitions) supports the physical phenomenon (quantum phase transition) by showing that A review of quantum phase transitions notes that such transitions occur at zero temperature when a non‑thermal control parameter such as pressure or magnetic field is varied.
external_sources.remaining_gap: While the cited source provides partial support, a dedicated mathematical or physical proof specific to the Trawinistic framework is still required to fully establish this entry.
external_sources.relevance: A review of quantum phase transitions notes that such transitions occur at zero temperature when a non‑thermal control parameter such as pressure or magnetic field is varied. Order is destroyed purely by quantum fluctuations rooted in the Heisenberg uncertainty principle. This supports the idea that quantum phase transitions on a Trawinistic manifold occur when geometric or coupling parameters cross critical values.
